label: llamacpp-qwen36-27b-q4_0-sycl-tp4-assist005-sg2-p512-n512

validatedRun.commandSnippet: ONEAPI_DEVICE_SELECTOR=level_zero:2,1,3,0 GGML_SYCL_DISABLE_GRAPH=0 GGML_SYCL_Q8_CACHE=1 GGML_SYCL_FUSE_MMVQ2=1 GGML_SYCL_ASYNC_CPY_TENSOR=1 GGML_SYCL_COMM_ALLREDUCE=1 GGML_SYCL_COMM_SINGLE_KERNEL=1 GGML_SYCL_COMM_EVENT_BARRIER=1 GGML_SYCL_COMM_SYNC_AFTER=2 GGML_META_FUSE_ALLREDUCE_ADD=1 GGML_SYCL_REORDER_MMVQ_SUBGROUPS_RUNTIME=2 /home/steve/src/llama.cpp-q4-b70/build-sycl-2026-bmg-g31/bin/llama-bench -m /home/steve/models/qwen3.6-27b-q4_0-gguf/Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_0.gguf -ngl 99 -fa 1 -ctk f16 -ctv f16 -dev SYCL0/SYCL1/SYCL2/SYCL3 -sm tensor -ts 1/1/1/0.05 -b 2048 -ub 32 --poll 50 -t 8 -p 512 -n 512 -r 3 -o jsonl

localMaxxing.firstAttemptIssue: HTTP 400 because the API rejected backend value sycl-level-zero; accepted retry omitted backend and preserved details in notes.

interpretation: A small fourth-card assist split improves the current four-card Q4_0 GGUF path, but equal four-way splitting is still inefficient. The fourth B70 is healthy; the bottleneck is likely reordered Q4_0 MMVQ efficiency on narrow row shards plus residual communication/synchronization overhead. The best production-quality Q4_0 path remains three B70s until deeper kernel/graph changes land.
